name: BTT 16 Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos Trail
name_original: Sierra de Loarre – circular desde el Castillo de Loarre
description: This moderate 18.7-mile mountain biking trail takes you through the Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos, passing Loarre Castle and the
region_intro: Mountain biking around Loarre offers diverse routes through a landscape characterized by the foothills of the Pyrenees, historic castle views, and varied natural terrain. The region features a mix of open areas, forested sections, and elevated viewpoints. Riders can expect trails that navigate both the Sierra de Loarre and the dramatic formations of the Reino de los Mallos. This area provides a compelling backdrop for mountain biking adventures. Best mountain bike trails around Loarre The most popular mountain bike trail is BTT 16 Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos Trail, a 18.7 miles (30.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain across the Sierra de Loarre and the unique Reino de los Mallos. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Loarre Castle – Loarre Castle loop from Loarre, a difficult 15.6 miles (25.1 km) path. This loop provides challenging climbs and descents with views of the historic Loarre Castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Loarre Castle – Vultures Viewpoint loop from Loarre, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with a notable viewpoint, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Loarre is defined by mountainous terrain, historic castle views, and scenic vantage points. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Loarre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Loarre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Loarre?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails documented around Loarre on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Loarre?
A: The mountain bike trails around Loarre are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no easy routes specifically listed. The terrain often involves significant elevation changes and technical sections, making it more suitable for riders with some experience. For family outings, consider exploring other activities in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Loarre?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Loarre is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at midday, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, limiting some routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic viewpoints in Loarre?
A: Yes, many trails offer stunning views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle – Vultures Viewpoint loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt; leads to a notable viewpoint. Other attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/324002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riglos Griffon Vulture Viewing Area&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4630341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colorado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible and offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape, including the dramatic Mallos de Riglos and Agüero.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Loarre?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Loarre are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle – Loarre Castle loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 26.1 km circular route that offers extensive views and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Loarre?
A: The terrain around Loarre is characterized by the foothills of the Pyrenees, featuring a mix of open areas, forested sections, and mountainous paths. Riders will encounter varied surfaces, including rocky sections, dirt tracks, and some paved stretches, often with significant climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any advanced mountain bike trails near Loarre Castle?
A: Yes, several challenging routes are available near Loarre Castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle – Loarre Castle loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 39.8 km trail with nearly 950 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding ride with views of the historic castle.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Loarre?
A: The mountain bike trails in Loarre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Riders often praise the stunning views of Loarre Castle, the challenging and varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore the unique natural formations of the Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos.

Q: What natural monuments or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Loarre?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de Riglos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, which are striking rock formations. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcuello Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent features along many routes.

Q: Are there options for winter mountain biking in Loarre?
A: While some lower elevation trails might be accessible, winter mountain biking in Loarre can be challenging due to colder temperatures and the possibility of snow, especially at higher altitudes in the Sierra de Loarre. It's advisable to check local weather and trail conditions before planning a winter ride.

Q: Where can I find parking at mountain bike trailheads in Loarre?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Loarre, which serves as a common starting point for many trails, including those leading to Loarre Castle. Specific parking details for individual trailheads may vary, so it's recommended to check the route details on komoot for the most precise information.
tips: Only the views of the Castle of Loarre already impress. On windless days, you will also be lucky to see it surrounded by people paragliding. But it is worth visiting. It is the best-preserved Romanesque castle in the world and was also the setting for the film 'The Kingdom of Heaven', by Ridley Scott.

faqs.question: Who is the BTT 16 Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos Trail best suited for?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as 'Black' difficulty with a 'Hard' physical rating, making it suitable for advanced mountain bikers. It demands high technical skill and physical endurance due to significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied. You'll encounter technical uphill and downhill paths on land and rock, well-maintained tracks through cultivated fields and forests, and sections with conglomerate terrain and loose stones. The route traverses pine and holm oak forests, Mediterranean scrub, and humid Pyrenean forests.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the BTT 16 Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily starts from Loarre Castle. Parking facilities are typically available near the castle, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass several notable landmarks and natural features. Highlights include the starting point at the historic Loarre Castle, the unique geological formations of the Mallos (like Mallos de Riglos), and the picturesque Fonfría Shelter and spring. The route also offers panoramic views of the Pyrenees and the Hoya de Huesca region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Autumn, winter, and spring are ideal for riding this trail. While summer riding is possible, it's best done in the evenings or on north-facing routes to avoid the heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride the BTT 16 Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or fees required to ride this mountain biking trail itself. However, the trail is located within the Paisaje Protegido de San Juan de la Peña y Monte Oroel, so it's always good practice to be aware of any general regulations for protected areas.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for mountain biking within the Paisaje Protegido de San Juan de la Peña y Monte Oroel?
faqs.answer: When riding within the Paisaje Protegido de San Juan de la Peña y Monte Oroel, it's important to stick to marked trails, respect wildlife, and avoid disturbing the natural environment. Always practice Leave No Trace principles, carry out all your trash, and be mindful of other trail users. Specific regulations regarding access or activities might be posted at trailheads or on local authority websites.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region allow dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain, potential for wildlife encounters, and the presence of other trail users. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the extensive 'Reino de los Mallos Bike' network. It also shares sections with other trails, including the Camino Natural de la Hoya de Huesca, BTT 13 Sierra de Loarre - Mirador de los Pirineos, and the Camino de Santiago por Huesca.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The BTT 16 Sierra de Loarre and Reino de los Mallos Trail is approximately 30 kilometers long. For an experienced mountain biker, it typically takes around 3 hours and 18 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The region around Loarre Castle and the Mallos is popular with tourists and outdoor enthusiasts. You'll find charming villages nearby that offer cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options, particularly in towns like Loarre or those closer to the Mallos de Riglos.
